pr.title: ADRs 0001–0003: canonical execution-attempt / execution-record / Revit tool boundary (proposals, no code change)
pr.description:
## Summary

Phase 0 of the Chief Architect roadmap: three **decision-record proposals** that name the canonical "machine" for each duplicate pair the loop must traverse, so the real SetParameterValue loop is driven through one unambiguous path. **Docs only — no runtime code, no deletion, no rename, no rewiring.** Retirement/loop-dependent calls are explicitly marked *pending furnace evidence*. Introduces a new `docs/architecture/decisions/` folder (no prior ADR convention existed).

Decisions (evidence-backed; full evidence tables in each file):

- **ADR-0001 — execution attempt:** v1 and v2 both export a class named `ExecutionAttemptEngine`. The M4 chain imports **v2** (`execution_chain_orchestrator.py:47`); v1 is off-chain (Work Prioritization, PR #111) and produces no artifacts, v2 is on-chain (Execution Step, PR #139) with `artifacts/execution_attempt_v2/` present. → **v2 canonical; v1 retire-candidate pending furnace evidence.**
- **ADR-0002 — execution record:** chain writes **`ExecutionReport`** (`orchestrator.py:49,521`, PR #142); `ExecutionTrace`/`persistence.py` (initial-foundation SQLite) is off-chain. → **ExecutionReport canonical for the loop; ExecutionTrace retained as active adapter/job store — not legacy.**
- **ADR-0003 — Revit tool boundary:** real named-pipe bridge (`pipe_client`+`automation_bridge`, `simulate` fallback off-Windows) vs mock `mcp_layer.py`. → **pipe bridge is the canonical real boundary (`simulate=False` on Windows); MCP off-path, not retired** (per local-first / no-MCP rule). Real-mutation leg unproven → pending furnace evidence.

## What did NOT change
- No source code, no atlas structure, no CLI, no tests. No component deleted, renamed, or rewired. These are proposals awaiting Chief Architect approval; any future retirement is a separate, per-ADR, post-Phase-1 action.

## Tests run
- None — docs-only change (per tiered-testing policy). No Revit live validation required; 2024 baseline unaffected.

## Factory relevance
Strengthens evidence quality / anti-duplication by fixing the single canonical execution path the verification-factory loop runs through, preventing future PRs from re-forking attempt/record/boundary concepts.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev
